commands.review_summary:
The PR changes IRC fallback nick generation in `extensions/irc/src/client.ts` from a fixed underscore suffix to a module-level monotonically increasing suffix.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible: current main uses a fixed underscore fallback and rebuilds fallback state for fresh IRC client connections, matching the linked issue mechanism. I did not run a live IRC/ngircd reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is the right lane because the external PR needs real behavior proof and maintainer choice between sibling fix candidates, not an automated repair job.

target.title: fix(irc): prevent ghost nick collisions on rejoin after network delay
target.body:
### Summary

- When an IRC connection experiences a network delay and rejoins, the nick collision fallback always appends the same suffix , causing a ghost nick collision with the still-connected session.
- This fix adds a monotonically increasing sequence counter to the fallback nick suffix, so each reconnection attempt generates a unique nick (e.g., , ).
- IRC spec requires unique nicks per connection; reusing  guarantees a collision every time.

### Linked issue

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/96064

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Repeated IRC reconnections after network delay fail because every fallback nick is identical ().
- **Real environment tested:** Unit test verification (see below). The IRC extension requires a live IRC server for end-to-end testing, which is not available in this environment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. pnpm build — clean
  2. pnpm test extensions/irc/ — 2 passed, 1 skipped (live test)
- **Evidence after fix:** Unit tests pass. The  function now returns unique suffixes for each sequential call: first call returns , second returns , third returns , etc.
- **Observed result after fix:**  returns , , , ... on successive calls.
- **What was not tested:** Live IRC server reconnection scenario.

### Tests and validation

``'
pnpm build
pnpm test extensions/irc/
``'

### Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change: No (fixes a connection reliability bug)
- Config/default surface change: No
- Security/audit impact: No
- New dependency: No
- Migration needed: No

---

AI-assisted code generation used for this fix.
